report_name: Chinese APT FamousSparrow Hits Azerbaijan Energy Sector with Deed RAT

threat_actors.name: FamousSparrow
threat_actors.description: A sophisticated China-linked APT group that targeted an oil and gas company in Azerbaijan for cyber-espionage. The group exploited Microsoft Exchange vulnerabilities to deploy Deed RAT.
threat_actors.goals: Theft of sensitive information related to energy production, exploration data, pricing strategies, and contracts with European partners.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Gained initial access by exploiting vulnerabilities in a public-facing Microsoft Exchange server, likely the ProxyNotShell chain.

attack_patterns.name: Web Shell
attack_patterns.description: Deployed web shells on the compromised Microsoft Exchange server to establish a persistent foothold for command and control.

attack_patterns.name: DLL Search Order Hijacking
attack_patterns.description: Used an advanced DLL sideloading technique with a two-stage trigger to load the Deed RAT payload, evading automated analysis.

attack_patterns.name: Match Legitimate Name or Location
attack_patterns.description: Masqueraded the Deed RAT payload by hiding it in a folder path designed to mimic a legitimate LogMeIn Hamachi installation (`C:\Program Files (x86)\LogMeIn Hamachi\`).

attack_patterns.name: Remote Desktop Protocol
attack_patterns.description: Used the Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) for lateral movement within the victim's network after the Deed RAT was active.

attack_patterns.name: Web Protocols
attack_patterns.description: The Deed RAT used HTTPS over port 443 for command and control communications to blend in with legitimate traffic.

malware.name: Deed RAT
malware.description: An updated variant of the Deed RAT was deployed as the final payload. It was loaded via DLL sideloading and used for persistence and cyber-espionage, with C2 communications over HTTPS.

tools.name: Remote Desktop Protocol
tools.description: Used by the FamousSparrow group for lateral movement within the compromised network.

vulnerabilities.name: ProxyNotShell
vulnerabilities.description: A Microsoft Exchange exploit chain, likely ProxyNotShell, was used to gain initial access to the victim's network.

identities.name: Unnamed oil and gas company in Azerbaijan
identities.description: A major oil and gas company in Azerbaijan targeted by the FamousSparrow APT group in a cyber-espionage campaign.

infrastructure.name: Deed RAT C2 Domain
infrastructure.description: Command and control infrastructure for the Deed RAT, using the domain sentinelonepro.com to masquerade as a legitimate security vendor.

indicators.name: Deed RAT C2 Domain
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Deed RAT C2 DNS Query
id: 2b9e1c4a-8d3f-4e9a-b9c1-7d8e9f0a1b2c
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries to the Deed RAT C2 domain sentinelonepro.com, which masquerades as a security vendor.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/news/bitdefender-uncovers-famoussparrow-attacks-on-azerbaijan-energy-sector-using-dll-sideloading-deed-rat-malware/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-15
tags:
  - attack.t1071.001
  - attack.command_and_control
falsepositives:
  - Unlikely.
level: high
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName: 'sentinelonepro.com'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network traffic to the C2 domain sentinelonepro.com used by Deed RAT.

indicators.name: FamousSparrow Masquerading File Path
indicators.pattern:
title: FamousSparrow Masquerading File Path
id: 8c3d4e5f-6a7b-8901-cdef-123456789012
status: experimental
description: Detects processes executing from a folder path mimicking a LogMeIn Hamachi installation, a TTP used by FamousSparrow to hide the Deed RAT.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/news/bitdefender-uncovers-famoussparrow-attacks-on-azerbaijan-energy-sector-using-dll-sideloading-deed-rat-malware/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-15
tags:
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.defense_evasion
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate LogMeIn Hamachi installations. Filter if this software is used in the environment.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|startswith: 'C:\Program Files (x86)\LogMeIn Hamachi\'
  filter_legit:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\hamachi-2.exe'
      - '\hamachi-2-ui.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit
indicators.description: Detects processes executing from a folder path (`C:\Program Files (x86)\LogMeIn Hamachi\`) used by FamousSparrow to masquerade its Deed RAT payload.

indicators.name: Potential FamousSparrow Web Shell on Exchange Server
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential FamousSparrow Web Shell on Exchange Server
id: 9d4e5f6a-7b8c-9012-def0-123456789012
status: experimental
description: Detects suspicious .aspx files in Microsoft Exchange OWA authentication directories, a potential indicator of web shells deployed by groups like FamousSparrow.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/news/bitdefender-uncovers-famoussparrow-attacks-on-azerbaijan-energy-sector-using-dll-sideloading-deed-rat-malware/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-15
tags:
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.persistence
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate .aspx files in this path, though uncommon.
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|contains|all:
      - '\owa\auth\'
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.aspx'
  filter_known_good:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '\logon.aspx'
      - '\error.aspx'
      - '\expiredpassword.aspx'
  condition: selection and not filter_known_good
indicators.description: Detects suspicious ASPX files in Exchange OWA directories, a common location for web shells deployed after exploiting vulnerabilities like ProxyNotShell.

relationships.target_name: Unnamed oil and gas company in Azerbaijan

relationships.source_name: Unnamed oil and gas company in Azerbaijan
Potential FamousSparrow Web Shell on Exchange Server
